items.title: Populist billionaire Andrej Babis campaigned on cutting aid to Ukraine | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Billionaire and former Prime Minister Andrej Babish's right-wing ANO party has clinched victory in the Czech parliamentary election. With nearly all ballots counted, the party won almost 35 percent of the vote. The Spolu alliance of Prime Minister Petr Fiala is far behind with just 23 percent. Six parties are above the five percent threshold required to win seats in the lower house. The victory is likely to strengthen the populist, anti-immigration camp in Europe and reduce Czech support for Ukraine. But his party will need to form a coalition with other parties. Interview with journalist Ian Willoughby in Prague.

#babis #czechia #czechrepublic 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: US envoys head to Egypt for talks with Israel and Hamas | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US special Middle East envoy Steve Witkoff is expected to arrive in Cairo later today to take part in negotiations between Israel and Hamas. The indirect talks on Sunday are taking place after Hamas agreed to parts of Donald Trump's ceasefire proposal. The US president urged Israel to halt its bombing of Gaza after Hamas said it was ready to release all remaining hostages. But the militant group said some elements of the deal need further examination. In a social media post, Trump told Hamas to move quickly or else all bets will be off. How likely is it that Israel and Hamas will keep to their part of the deal? Perspectives from journalist Karim El-Gawhary in Cairo, Professor Sultan Barakat from Hamad Bin Khalifa University in Doha, and DW correspondent Emily Gordine.

00:45 Interview Karim El-Gawhary, Journalist
05:16 Interview Sultan Barakat, Professor at Hamad Bin Khalifa University
12:05 Interview Emily Gordine, DW correspondent

#israel #hamas #witkoff

items.title: Nine months after Assad, can Damascus deliver a true and fair democracy? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Syrians are preparing to vote in the first parliamentary elections since the fall of the Assad regime. The country is still reeling from over a decade of civil war that ended with the outsting of President Bashar al Assad by Islamist insurgents in December. The election results will be taken as a barometer of how serious the interim government of President Ahmad al-Sharaa is about respecting the rights of religious minorities and women. An interview with Dr. Bente Scheller, Head of Middle East and North Africa Division at the Heinrich Böll Foundation.

#syria #elections #ahmedalsharaa 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Hamas agrees to some of Trump's peace plan but wants negotiations | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Hamas says it has agreed to parts of Donald Trump's peace plan for Gaza, including the release of all remaining Israeli hostages, whether dead or alive. The US president has told Israel to stop bombing Gaza "immediately" and says he believes Hamas is ready for peace. But the militant group says some elements of the deal need further negotiation. 

00:00 Hamas agrees to free all hostages
02:00 DW's Emily Gordine reports from Jerusalem
05:48 Journalist Karim El-Gawhary reports from Cairo
09:54 DW speaks with Fawaz Gerges, professor of Middle East Politics and International Relations at the London School of Economics

items.title: Sanae Takaichi to become Japan's first female prime minister | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party has elected Sanae Takaichi as its new leader. The China hawk won the leadership vote putting her on course to become Japan's first female prime minister. She is charged with reviving her ailing party as a new anti-immigration grouping makes gains in the polls. Takaichi beat her closest rival in a run-off after none of the five candidates won a majority in the first round. A vote in parliament to elect the next prime minister is expected to take place later this month.

00:00 Japan's ruling party chooses new leader
00:35 DW speaks with journalist Sonja Blaschke in Tokyo

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Furious demonstrators are demanding an end to the political elite's lucrative benefits | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thousands of people in Pakistan-administered Kashmir gathered for the funeral of those killed in recent clashes with police. According to the local government, six civilians and three police officers have been killed in clashes between protesters and security forces since Monday. Protesters have demanded electoral reforms and an end to the lucrative benefits enjoyed by the political elite.

items.title: Thirty-five years after reunification, how has Germany's global role evolved? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Over the past 35 years, Germans have become reacquainted with each other in many ways.  Overcoming decades of division has been an enormous task, guided by only five chancellors. The country's sense of self within Europe and around the world has undergone a profound transformation.  This process has been welcomed by some but not by all. 

Chapters:
0:00 Germany celebrates 35 years since reunification
1:48 Jackson Janes, German Marshall Fund

items.title: Which coalition scenarios are most likely, and what effect would they have on priorities? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>People in the Czech Republic are choosing a new parliament in a two-day election. The outcome could dramatically change the country's political direction.
The current frontrunner, Andrej Babiš, is a populist who is critical of the European Union and the aid given to Ukraine. However, even if his party wins the most votes, it will likely need to form a coalition with other parties.

Chapters:
0:00 Czech Republic holds crucial parliamentary election
0:27 Haris Jeelani Toogo, Journalist

items.title: What was the reaction of Combs and his entourage to the verdict? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Sean "Diddy" Combs, an American hip-hop mogul, has been sentenced to more than four years in prison on prostitution-related charges. The 55-year-old was also fined $500,000 after being convicted in July of transporting people across state lines for sexual encounters. However, jurors acquitted him of racketeering conspiracy and sex trafficking charges, which could have resulted in a life sentence.

Chapters:
0:00 Sean 'Diddy' Combs sentenced to four years in prison
0:28 Lisa Bonner, Entertainment lawyer and former litigator

items.title: What role do Venezuela and President Maduro play in the international drug trade? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The U.S. announced that it carried out a new strike on a boat off the coast of Venezuela — the fourth in recent weeks. This comes after President Donald Trump declared war on drug cartels. Trump made this designation in a notice sent to Congress on Wednesday that has since been seen by multiple media outlets.

items.title: Are German's hearts and minds united? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thirty-five years have passed since the reunification of West and East Germany. Today, Chancellor Friedrich Merz marked the anniversary by warning that liberal democracies, including Germany, are under attack from both inside and outside the country. French President Emmanuel Macron was also present, urging Germany to take on a larger role in Europe.

Chapters:
0:00 Germany marks 35 years since reunification
2:55 Alex Gerst, DW Political Correspondent

items.title: Hamas also 'willing to hand over administration of Gaza' | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Hamas has agreed to release all remaining Israeli hostages, under US President's Donald Trump's peace plan for Gaza. 
The militant group also said that it is willing to hand over administration of the Gaza Strip to an independent body of Palestinian technocrats. However, Hamas said that some elements of Trump's plan still need further negotiation. The group added that it appreciated Arab, Islamic, and international efforts to bring about an agreement. Trump had given Hamas until Sunday evening to accept the deal.

Chapters:
0:00 Hamas agrees to release all hostages dead or alive
0:52 Fawaz Gerges, Middle East analyst

items.title: Will Israel face global fallout over its interception of Gaza aid flotilla? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A large-scale civilian humanitarian mission has failed to reach its stated goal of delivering symbolic aid to Gaza and breaking the Israeli siege of the territory. Israeli forces intercepted the last vessel sailing towards Gaza today - and have arrested around 450 activists. The convoy had been sailing in international waters, prompting world leaders, rights groups and lawyers to accuse Israel of violating maritime law. Protests have also been staged in cities arund the world. We spoke to journalists Alina Trabattoni in Rome, and Nathan Morley in Cyprus.

#israel #gaza #flotilla
